text:
There once was a lonely cat named Mortimer who lived in a lush forest. Every dawn, Mortimer would sit by his favorite crack in the walnut tree and gaze silently at the dew-covered leaves. It was his life—he alone amongst the trees—but something was missing. Each day he dreamt of having a friend.

One morning, while lazily blinking the morning light into clarity, Mortimer noticed something peculiar. A small squirrel with a bushy tail was descending from high up the oak tree. Mortimer watched it gather acorns and then scamper away up the trunk.

Curious, Mortimer climbed up after the squirrel. As he neared the bough where the little critter had vanished, a branch swayed ominously. Mortimer leaped back just in time—his claws clinging onto nothing but air. He had startled a squirrel! Thinking quickly, he scampered back down and began a silent charm.

"A friend?" Mortimer imagined the squirrel saying. His tiny voice seemed
